summary: Checked 27 claims, found 4 mirages (2 critical, 2 major). Round 2 re-validation after round-1 fixes.

findings.claim: tech-spec Architecture 'How it works' flow says: switchViewToProcPidStat builds a view.View with CollectExtra/IOAvailable set, then calls 'viewSwitchHandler(config, view)' which 'sends view on config.viewCh'. Task 6 description repeats: 'build a view.View ... then call viewSwitchHandler'.
findings.reality: viewSwitchHandler(config *config, c string) at top/config_view.go:188 takes a STRING view name, not a view.View. It internally does: config.view = config.views[c]; config.viewCh <- config.view. If switchViewToProcPidStat builds a custom view.View with CollectExtra and IOAvailable set and then calls viewSwitchHandler('procpidstat'), the function will OVERWRITE config.view from the stored config.views['procpidstat'] entry — silently discarding the CollectExtra and IOAvailable fields that were just set.
findings.source: 001-feat-per-process-system-stats-tech-spec.md, Architecture section 'How it works' flow diagram; Task 6 description
findings.fix: Either (a) before calling viewSwitchHandler, update config.views['procpidstat'] with the modified fields (so viewSwitchHandler picks them up), or (b) bypass viewSwitchHandler entirely and directly set config.view with the modified fields and send on config.viewCh. Update the tech-spec flow diagram and Task 6 to reflect the actual viewSwitchHandler(config *config, c string) signature.

findings.claim: tech-spec says: 'itv source: elapsed duration between current and previous Update() call, in seconds, already computed in the existing Update() loop (used for diskstats/netdev).'
findings.reality: The itv in Collector.Update() is `itv := int(refresh / time.Second)` (stat.go:162) — it is the configured refresh interval, not elapsed wall-clock time. Diskstats and netdev do NOT use this itv: diskstats computes `itv = curr[i].Uptime - prev[i].Uptime` (diskstats.go:207) and netdev does the same (netdev.go:193). The Update()-level itv is passed only to calculateDelta for SQL-level diffs. The claim 'already computed ... used for diskstats/netdev' is factually incorrect for both the source and the consumers.
findings.source: 001-feat-per-process-system-stats-tech-spec.md, Architecture section 'itv source' paragraph; internal/stat/stat.go:162; internal/stat/diskstats.go:207; internal/stat/netdev.go:193
findings.fix: Correct the description: procpidstat will use `itv = int(refresh / time.Second)` from the Update() refresh parameter (which is different from the uptime-delta itv used by diskstats/netdev). Remove the false claim. Optionally, adopt the uptime-delta pattern from diskstats for higher accuracy — but either choice must be stated correctly in the tech-spec.

findings.claim: tech-spec SQL constant uses template convention '{{if ne .QueryAgeThresh ""}}AND query_start < now() - '{{.QueryAgeThresh}}'::interval{{end}}', stated to follow 'the exact conventions of the existing activity query (use {{if ne .QueryAgeThresh ""}} not {{if gt ...}})'.
findings.reality: The existing activity queries (internal/query/activity.go:14-15, 27-28, 40-41) never use an {{if ne .QueryAgeThresh ""}} guard — they embed QueryAgeThresh unconditionally in a WHERE clause: "WHERE ((clock_timestamp() - xact_start) > '{{.QueryAgeThresh}}'::interval ...". This works because QueryAgeThresh is NEVER empty: its default is '00:00:00.0' (query.go:43), which acts as a no-filter threshold. The {{if ne .QueryAgeThresh ""}} guard is therefore always true in practice and is not the pattern used anywhere in the codebase. The tech-spec's SQL query structure (an optional AND appended to 'WHERE pid != pg_backend_pid()') also differs semantically from the existing WHERE-only pattern.
findings.source: 001-feat-per-process-system-stats-tech-spec.md, Data Models 'New SQL constant'; Task 2 description; internal/query/activity.go:14-15; internal/query/query.go:43
findings.fix: Use the actual existing convention: embed QueryAgeThresh unconditionally without an {{if ne}} guard, relying on the default '00:00:00.0' to be a no-op filter. Example: 'AND (clock_timestamp() - query_start) > '{{.QueryAgeThresh}}'::interval'. Update Task 2 instruction accordingly.

findings.claim: tech-spec Decision 1 describes CollectExtra on View as 'parallel to how CollectDiskstats / CollectNetdev work' with the same mechanism.
findings.reality: The existing CollectDiskstats/CollectNetdev mechanism stores the flag in view.ShowExtra, which collectStat (top/stat.go:32,82-83) reads and passes to c.ToggleCollectExtra(), which sets c.config.collectExtra; Collector.Update() then switches on c.config.collectExtra. The proposed view.CollectExtra is a genuinely different field: it would be read directly from the view argument in Collector.Update() (not via ToggleCollectExtra/c.config.collectExtra). The collectStat goroutine has no special handling for view.CollectExtra changes. Describing this as 'parallel' misstates the mechanism and could cause implementers to incorrectly hook into the ToggleCollectExtra path.
findings.fix: Correct Decision 1 to state explicitly that view.CollectExtra is a new, separate field from view.ShowExtra, that Collector.Update() switches on view.CollectExtra (the view argument) directly — NOT via c.config.collectExtra or ToggleCollectExtra — and that this is a deliberately different path to avoid triggering the side-panel creation in top/ui.go.
